summary: Scorching Blow Away, tight Scarlet>Fire, high‑energy set throughout
blurb: The concert erupts with a soaring Blow Away and a flawless Let It Grow, while the second set delivers a blistering Scarlet Begonias > Fire on the Mountain and The Other One. Jerry’s guitar is on fire, Brent shines with an extended solo, and the band maintains relentless momentum.
review: July 7, 1989 at John F. Kennedy Stadium captured one of the Dead’s late‑’80s peaks. From the opening “Hell In A Bucket” through a raucous “Iko Iko,” the first set built to an unforgettable climax with a soaring, extended version of “Blow Away” that featured Brent Mydland’s twelve‑minute keyboard solo – a true heart‑stopper. The second half kept the fire burning: a seamless “Scarlet Begonias > Fire on the Mountain” segue, a tight “Estimated Prophet,” and an exploratory “The Other One/Wharf Rat” stretch that showcased the band’s improvisational chemistry. Jerry Garcia’s tone was described as perfect and scorching throughout, Phil Lesh laid down a beautiful, clear bass foundation, while Bob Weir kept solid rhythm despite occasional vocal criticism on “Estimated.” Brent’s keyboards were the night’s highlight, tearing it up on “Blow Away” and adding soulful color elsewhere. The crowd‑energy captured on several front‑of‑house audience tapes feels almost tactile, making this a must‑listen performance for any Deadhead.

rating_calculation.sentiment_rationale: Eight out of twelve recordings are strongly positive, repeatedly praising the soaring Blow Away, flawless Let It Grow, blistering Scarlet>Fire and Brent's extended solo. Mixed and negative notes focus on recording quality rather than performance. Consistent high praise across multiple sources yields a solid positive sentiment adjustment.

best_recording.reason: Crystal‑clear front‑of‑house audience tape that captures the show’s energy with superb dynamics and no dropouts, widely praised as the best Aud source.

key_highlights: Extended, soaring Blow Away with a ~12‑minute Brent Mydland keyboard solo
Seamless Scarlet Begonias > Fire on the Mountain transition that keeps the set’s momentum high
The Other One/Wharf Rat marathon showcasing deep improvisational chemistry
Jerry Garcia’s perfect guitar tone throughout, especially on the second‑set jams

band_performance.Jerry: Garcia delivers scorching, perfectly‑timed guitar work all night, with his tone described as flawless on both sets.
band_performance.Phil: Lesh’s bass is beautiful and clear, providing a solid foundation that underpins the band’s high‑energy flow.
band_performance.Bob: Weir supplies steady rhythm and contributes vocal parts; his vocals on “Estimated Prophet” were noted as slightly affected by some reviewers, but overall his playing remains reliable.
band_performance.Brent: Mydland shines with an epic ~12‑minute solo during “Blow Away,” tears it up across the set, and adds soulful keyboard textures that lift the performance.
